name: airlock-cursor-enforcer
description: Airlock enforcement layer for Cursor IDE — gates agent/terminal commands through mobile approval. Requires 'Run Everything as Agent' run mode in Cursor settings for full enforcement.

airlock.diagnosticMode.description: Enable verbose diagnostic logging. When enabled, detailed detection and IPC logs are written to the output channel. Default is off for production.

airlock.autoApprovePatterns.description: Commands matching any of these patterns are auto-approved without sending to the Gateway. Supports literal substrings and /regex/ patterns. Empty by default (all commands require approval).

airlock.approvalTimeoutSeconds.description: Timeout in seconds for waiting for mobile approval.

airlock.approvalEndpoint.description: Airlock approval endpoint URL (e.g. http://127.0.0.1:7771). If empty, auto-discovery is used.

airlock.hookBeforeSubmitPrompt.description: Enable the beforeSubmitPrompt hook. When enabled, every prompt submission is sent for mobile approval. Disabled by default because Cursor does not reliably respect this hook's output in all versions.

airlock.hookStop.description: Enable the stop hook. When enabled, agent stop events are sent through the gate script (informational only — Cursor ignores the hook output for stop events). Disabled by default.

airlock.hookSessionStart.description: Enable the sessionStart hook. When enabled, agent session lifecycle start events are sent through the gate script. Disabled by default.

airlock.hookSubagentStart.description: Enable the subagentStart hook. When enabled, subagent (Task tool) start events are sent through the gate script. Disabled by default.

airlock.hookBeforeReadFile.description: Enable the beforeReadFile hook. When enabled, file read operations are sent through the gate script for approval. Useful for protecting sensitive files. Disabled by default.

airlock.allowSelfSignedCerts.description: Allow self-signed or untrusted TLS certificates when connecting to the Airlock Gateway. Enable only for local development (e.g. Aspire dev certificates). Default: false (strict TLS verification).

scripts.pretest: npm run compile && npm run lint
